content.problemStatement: Given an directed graph with **N** nodes (numbered from 1 to **N**) and **M** edges, calculate the number of unordered pairs** (X, Y)** such there exist two paths, one from node 1 to node **X**, and another one from node 1 to node **Y**, such that they don't share any node except node 1.
constraints: The graph is a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG)  i.e. there is no cycle in the graph.
content.inputFormat:
There is only one test case in one test file.

The first line of each test case contains two space separated integers **N, M**. Each of the next M lines contains two space separated integers  **u, v** denoting a directed edge of graph G, from node **u** to node **v**. There are no multi-edges and self loops in the graph.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer corresponding to the number of unordered pairs as asked in the problem..
